title: Python, Tcl, Tkinter, Threads, and the Event Loop · Program Brief: Cooperative Events
summary: The interface creates multiple roots, blocks callbacks with work, calls widgets from an arbitrary thread, and exits with scheduled callbacks still alive. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cooperative events increment in single-root event-loop lifecycle contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Keep event handlers short and divide long generation or search work into bounded event-loop turns.
Create one application root, initialize state before mainloop, and shut down widgets and callbacks deliberately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-cooperative-events — Keep event handlers short and divide long generation or search work into bounded event-loop turns.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-runtime-tk-event-loop-4
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-runtime-tk-event-loop-4.
# Case 1655: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def worked_maze_runtime_tk_event_loop_4(roots: int = 1, pending_callbacks: int = 0, longest_callback_ms: int = 7, ui_thread_owned: bool = True, clean_shutdown: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "gridworked4",
        "topology1655",
        "search4dekrow",
        "eventworked41655",
        "access1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if roots != 1:
        return False, roots, "root-lifecycle"
    if pending_callbacks < 0:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "callback-count"
    if longest_callback_ms < 0 or longest_callback_ms > 16:
        return False, longest_callback_ms, "blocking-callback"
    if not ui_thread_owned or not clean_shutdown:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "runtime-ownership"
    assert roots == 1 and pending_callbacks == 0
    return True, longest_callback_ms, "cooperative-runtime"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-cooperative-events-1eafms
# Competency: maze-cooperative-events.
# Case 1388: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it.
def evidence_maze_cooperative_events_1eafms(roots: int = 1, pending_callbacks: int = 0, longest_callback_ms: int = 7, ui_thread_owned: bool = True, clean_shutdown: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1eafms = "1eafms-smfae1-1388"
    assert evidence_variant_1eafms
    evidence_axes_1eafms = (
        "grid1eafms",
        "topology1388",
        "searchsmfae1",
        "event1eafms1388",
        "access13881eafms",
        "recoverysmfae11388",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1eafms)) == 6
    if roots != 1:
        return False, roots, "root-lifecycle"
    if pending_callbacks < 0:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "callback-count"
    if longest_callback_ms < 0 or longest_callback_ms > 16:
        return False, longest_callback_ms, "blocking-callback"
    if not ui_thread_owned or not clean_shutdown:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "runtime-ownership"
    assert roots == 1 and pending_callbacks == 0
    return True, longest_callback_ms, "cooperative-runtime"

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Cooperative Events model · Theory Cooperative Events, keep Cooperative Events connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Keep event handlers short and divide long generation or search work into bounded event-loop turns.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Cooperative Events model · Theory Cooperative Events, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Cooperative Events precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Cooperative Events model · Theory Cooperative Events, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 2636 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to model a single-root cooperative event loop with bounded callbacks and deliberate shutdown; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep event handlers short and divide long generation or search work into bounded event-loop turns.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Cooperative Events model · Theory Cooperative Events, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A long callback is harmless because the interface has a mainloop.
